title: carex muricata subsp. lamprocarpa
intro: Carex muricata subsp. lamprocarpa, also known as the rough-stemmed sedge or golden sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Carex muricata subsp. lamprocarpa is typically harvested, dried, and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use.
